title: Signal tonal-vs-noise diagnostic sweep — PARTIAL support for the QA core orbit-structure claim with a parameter-region obstruction

observation.summary: The flagship final signal runner failed its pre-registered tonal-vs-noise criterion under the default configuration, with White Noise finishing above the tonal cases by harmonic index. A focused 576-configuration diagnostic sweep then found 165 passing configurations. The best region used num_nodes=16, modulus=24, coupling=0.12, noise_base=0.1, noise_annealing=0.995, signal_injection_strength=0.2, signal_mode=final, where Minor Chord=0.8582, Major Chord=0.8206, Pure Tone=0.7797, Tritone=0.0175, and White Noise=0.0016. Interpretation: the signal hypothesis is supported in a real nearby parameter region, but the default flagship setting remains an obstruction, so the support is conditional rather than global.

parent_cert.claim: state_space orbit structure reflects genuine temporal correlation structure rather than arbitrary labelling

evidence.description: A substantial nearby parameter region satisfied the pre-registered tonal-vs-noise criterion
The best final-mode configuration flipped the ordering in the intended direction
evidence.value: Minor Chord=0.8582, Major Chord=0.8206, Pure Tone=0.7797, White Noise=0.0016

evidence.description: The original flagship configuration failed strongly, showing the effect is parameter-sensitive rather than automatic
evidence.value: default White Noise=0.8286 vs Major Chord=0.0169 and Minor Chord=0.0139

evidence.description: The same family of QA dynamics can express both success and failure depending on the parameter region, which supports a narrower signal claim while warning against overgeneralization
evidence.value: best-run recovery plus default-run obstruction in the same session

open_questions: RESOLVED 2026-07-10 (NEGATIVELY): the best signal region does NOT remain stable across seeds. All key_numbers in this record were computed under the historical Harmonic Index HI = e8_alignment * exp(-0.1*loss), which was later shown to be non-discriminative (e8_alignment scored mean|cos| to a hardcoded Fibonacci vector, not E8; loss ~ 0 over every run, so HI was near-constant and its cross-signal ordering was noise). Under the redesigned discriminative HI (golden-orbit Kuramoto coherence; qa_core/metrics.py::harmonic_coherence, 2026-07-10) the tonal-vs-noise ordering does not survive: the 'final' flagship config REVERSES (seed-level paired t-test n=25, t=-3.68, p=0.0012, white noise significantly HIGHER), and the amplitude-normalized multiseed config is NULL (n=8, t=1.63, p=0.147; means all within ~0.003, win counts 3-5/8 near chance). See qa_signal_experiment_hi_rerun.py / experiment_protocol.json and signal_experiment_multiseed_eval.py.
Does the best signal region remain stable across multiple random seeds rather than a single deterministic sweep seed?
Can the flagship runner be frozen to a cert-worthy default without sacrificing robustness across signal families?
How much of the tonal-vs-noise separation is QA-specific versus recoverable by non-QA baselines under the same observer projection?

validation_checks.description: observation.source is a known source type
verdict is one of {CONSISTENT, CONTRADICTS, PARTIAL, INCONCLUSIVE}
verdict==CONTRADICTS implies nonempty fail_ledger (not applicable here)

superseded_note.reason: All HI values in this record used the non-discriminative historical Harmonic Index (e8_alignment to a hardcoded Fibonacci vector, loss ~ 0). The tonal-vs-noise ordering does not replicate under the redesigned discriminative coherence-HI: 'final' config reverses (n=25, t=-3.68, p=0.0012), multiseed config null (n=8, p=0.147). The PARTIAL verdict was metric-dependent and is retracted to INCONCLUSIVE for the tonal-vs-noise claim.
